headline: How Republicans weaponised climate investing
summary: Corporate governance reporter Patrick Temple-West answers a crucial question: is it time for an ESG rebrand?
body_text:
This is an audio transcript of the Behind the Money podcast episode: ‘How Republicans weaponised climate investing’

Michela TinderaIn the US, midterm elections are coming up in just a couple of weeks. And ahead of those there’s been this war of words popping up from Republicans in state capitals around the country. And we’re not talking about abortion rights or gun control or immigration. We’re talking about one acronym plucked straight from the world of finance — ESG, as in environmental, social and governance investing.

Patrick Temple-West Republicans broadly have identified ESG — environmental, social and governance investing — as a political issue.

Michela TinderaThat’s Patrick Temple-West. He’s the FT’s corporate governance reporter.

Patrick Temple-West Now, something that has been a small but growing segment of the financial sector, a way to screen companies for potential risks, potential opportunities, has become weaponised.
